Step 1: construct necessary inputs to while_loop based on scan's input.\\n587:             (\\n588:                 init,\\n589:                 xs,\\n590:                 additional_inputs,\\n591:             ) = pytree.tree_unflatten(args, tree_spec)\\n592:             scan_length = xs[0].size(0)\\n593:             loop_idx = torch.zeros([], dtype=torch.int64, device=torch.device(\\\"cpu\\\"))\\n594: \\n595:             # NOTE [Pre-allocate scan's output buffer]\\n596:             # In order to pre-allocate the output buffer for ys, we rely on the meta of scan's fx_node.\\n597:             # However, the meta consists of concrete symints, we need to bind those symints with\\n598:             # proxies in order to trace the torch.empty_strided call correctly.\\n599:             #\\n600:             # Also note that basic free symbols of tensor's shapes are guaranteed to be lifted as subgraph inputs\\n601:             # in dynamo so we can always re-construct the sym expression from placeholders.\\n602:             # See Note [Auto lift basic free symbols when create_graph_input] for how this is done.\\n603:             bound_symbols = {\\n604:                 arg.node.expr: arg\\n605:                 for arg in pytree.tree_leaves((args, scan_length))\\n606:                 if isinstance(arg, torch.SymInt)\\n607:             }\\n608:             ys_outs = [\\n609:                 torch.empty_strided(\\n610:                     resolve_shape_to_proxy(ys_out.size(), bound_symbols),\\n611:                     resolve_shape_to_proxy(ys_out.stride(), bound_symbols),\\n612:                     device=ys_out.device,\\n613:                     dtype=ys_out.dtype,\\n614:                     layout=ys_out.layout,\\n615:                     requires_grad=ys_out.requires_grad,\\n616:                 )\\n617:                 for ys_out in ys_outputs\\n618:             ]\\n619: \\n620:             while_loop_operands = (loop_idx, ys_outs, init, xs)\\n621:             flat_operands, operands_spec = pytree.tree_flatten(while_loop_operands)\\n622:             _, operands_and_additional_inputs_spec = pytree.tree_flatten(\\n623:                 (*while_loop_operands, additional_inputs)\\n624:             )\\n625: \\n626:             # Step 2: create the cond_fn and body_fn for while_loop\\n627:             def cond_fn(*flat_args):\\n628:                 loop_idx, _, _, _, _ = pytree.tree_unflatten(\\n629:                     flat_args, operands_and_additional_inputs_spec\\n630:                 )  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n631:                 return loop_idx < scan_length  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n632: \\n633:             def body_fn(*flat_args):\\n634:                 loop_idx, ys_outs, carry, xs, additional_inputs = pytree.tree_unflatten(\\n635:                     flat_args,\\n636:                     operands_and_additional_inputs_spec,  #
